name: Old Latin
notes: Old Latin (~240–75 BC). The literary language of Plautus, Ennius, and Cato. Key differences from Classical: (1) Richer diphthong inventory: /ej/, /ow/, /oj/ still distinct (later monophthongised to ī, ū, ū). (2) Rhotacism ongoing: intervocalic /s/ → /r/ not yet complete in all forms. (3) Final -d on ablatives. (4) /h/ probably stronger. (5) Same 10-vowel quantity system as Classical. Epigraphy: Praenestine fibula (if genuine), Duenos inscription, Forum cippus, SC de Bacchanalibus (186 BC). Source: Weiss (2020), Sihler (1995).
